title: Fridge Cheesecake - Banting-style
excerpt: Well, Banting has a way of creeping into all our lives. Especially just before summer, when we're trying to shake off the extra kilos. Now I have to admit that in the process, my sweet tooth doesn't budge. So, I had to make a plan. Well, here's my fridge cheesecake – as close to Banting as it gets …
html:
Well, Banting has a way of creeping into all our lives. Especially just before summer, when we're trying to shake off the extra kilos. Now I have to admit that in the process, my sweet tooth doesn't budge. So, I had to make a plan. Well, here's my fridge cheesecake – as close to Banting as it gets.

 

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 500 ml coconut
  • 50g butter
  • 2 tubs cream cheese
  • 250ml cream
  • 5 ml vanilla essence
  • 5ml Sweet-O powdered sweetener
  • 15 ml gelatine
  • 80 ml warm water
  • strawberries for decoration, soaked in Xylitol

 

NOW:

  1. melt the butter in the microwave and mix with the coconut
  2. spray a springform pan with non-stick spray and press the coconut firmly into the pan
  3. bake the crust for 10 minutes at 180 degrees celsius
  4. let the crust cool while you make the filling
  5. dissolve the gelatine in the warm water – microwave for 30 seconds if you're struggling
  6. beat the cream cheese until soft and add the Sweet-O, then beat further
  7. in a separate bowl, whip the cream to soft peaks and add it to the cream cheese mixture
  8. now stir the gelatine mixture into the cream cheese/cream mixture
  9. spoon it onto your coconut crust and place in the fridge to set – about 2 hours
  10. take it out of your springform pan and decorate with your strawberries or any berries of your choice.

I must say, it's not the real deal when it comes to cheesecake, but for weight loss on Banting, this one will bring some relief.
